questions.question_text: Which Article of the Indian Constitution provides for the formation of new States and alteration of areas, boundaries, or names of existing States?
questions.explanation: Article 3 empowers Parliament to form new States, increase/decrease the area of any State, and alter State boundaries or names — by an ordinary majority, not a special majority.
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question_text: The concept of 'Doctrine of Pleasure' in the Indian Constitution means:

options.a: The President can dismiss any civil servant at will
options.b: High Court judges hold office during the pleasure of the Chief Justice
options.c: Civil servants hold office during the pleasure of the President/Governor
options.d: The Council of Ministers serves at the pleasure of Parliament

questions.explanation: Article 310 states that civil servants (and defence services members) hold office during the pleasure of the President (or Governor for state services). However, Article 311 provides procedural safeguards against arbitrary dismissal.
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question_text:
With reference to the Parliament of India, consider the following statements:
1. The Speaker of the Lok Sabha can vote only in case of a tie.
2. The Speaker is elected by all members of Parliament.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct — the Speaker has a casting vote only when the House is equally divided (Article 100). Statement 2 is incorrect — the Speaker is elected only by members of the Lok Sabha, not both Houses.
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question_text: The 'Battle of Plassey' (1757) was significant primarily because it:

options.a: Led to the expulsion of the French from India
options.b: Marked the beginning of British political supremacy in Bengal
options.c: Established the East India Company's monopoly over trade
options.d: Led to the formation of the Subsidiary Alliance system

questions.explanation: The Battle of Plassey (1757) established British political control over Bengal by defeating Siraj-ud-Daula with the help of Mir Jafar. It gave the Company revenue rights over Bengal, beginning British political supremacy in India.
questions.subject: History

questions.question_text: Which of the following Committees is associated with the decentralization of economic power and planning in India?
questions.explanation: The G V K Rao Committee (1985) recommended decentralization of planning and greater devolution of power to PRIs. It called Panchayati Raj institutions 'grass without roots.' The Balwant Rai Mehta Committee (1957) first recommended a three-tier PRI system.
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question_text: Which Article of the Indian Constitution abolishes 'untouchability' and forbids its practice in any form?
questions.explanation: Article 17 abolishes untouchability and makes its practice a punishable offence. This is an absolute right — it applies to both state and private actions, unlike most fundamental rights which only bind the State.
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question_text: The Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY) replaced which of the following schemes?

options.a: National Agricultural Insurance Scheme (NAIS) and Modified NAIS only
options.b: NAIS, Modified NAIS, and Weather-based Crop Insurance Scheme

questions.explanation: PMFBY, launched in 2016, replaced NAIS (National Agricultural Insurance Scheme) and Modified NAIS. The Weather-based Crop Insurance Scheme (WBCIS) continues separately under a restructured framework.
questions.subject: Economy

questions.question_text:
Consider the following about the Ramsar Convention:
1. It deals specifically with wetlands of international importance.
2. India is not a signatory to the convention.
3. The Convention requires the designation of at least one wetland to the List of Wetlands of International Importance upon joining.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statements 1 and 3 are correct. The Ramsar Convention (1971) focuses exclusively on wetland conservation. India is a signatory (joined 1982) — Statement 2 is wrong. Countries must designate at least one wetland upon accession.
questions.subject: Environment

questions.question_text: Which of the following is/are considered a 'Greenhouse Gas' under the Kyoto Protocol?

options.a: Carbon dioxide, Methane, and Nitrous oxide only
options.c: Carbon dioxide, Methane, Nitrous oxide, HFCs, PFCs, and SF6

questions.explanation: The Kyoto Protocol covers six greenhouse gases: CO₂, CH₄ (methane), N₂O (nitrous oxide), HFCs, PFCs, and SF₆ (sulphur hexafluoride). The 2012 Doha Amendment added NF₃ as a seventh.
questions.subject: Environment

questions.question_text: The 'Tropic of Cancer' does NOT pass through which of the following Indian States?
questions.explanation: The Tropic of Cancer (23.5°N) passes through 8 Indian states: Gujarat, Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, West Bengal, Tripura, and Mizoram. Manipur lies further north and is NOT traversed.
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question_text: With reference to 'Inflation Targeting' in India, which of the following is correct?

options.a: The RBI sets the inflation target independently
options.b: The Government sets the inflation target in consultation with RBI, and the RBI is responsible for achieving it
options.c: Parliament sets the inflation target by a resolution
options.d: The Finance Commission sets the inflation target every five years

questions.explanation: Under the amended RBI Act (Section 45ZA), the Central Government determines the inflation target in consultation with RBI every five years. Currently the target is 4% CPI ±2%. The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is responsible for achieving it.
questions.subject: Economy

questions.question_text:
Which of the following pairs is NOT correctly matched?
(Satellite launch vehicle : Developing country)
questions.explanation: Falcon 9 is developed by SpaceX, a private American company (USA) — not Russia. Russia's main orbital launch vehicles include Soyuz and Proton. The other three pairings are correct.
questions.subject: Science & Technology

questions.question_text: The 'Galwan Valley' incident of 2020 occurred in which Union Territory?
questions.explanation: The Galwan Valley, the site of the June 2020 India-China clash, is located in the Ladakh Union Territory near the Line of Actual Control (LAC). Ladakh was bifurcated from Jammu & Kashmir as a separate UT in October 2019.
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question_text:
With reference to 'Mission Karmayogi', consider the following:
1. It is a capacity-building programme for civil servants.
2. It is implemented by the Department of Personnel and Training (DoPT).
3. It includes the iGOT (Integrated Government Online Training) Karmayogi platform.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: All three statements are correct. Mission Karmayogi (2020) is a national programme for civil servants' capacity building. It is anchored by DoPT and includes the iGOT Karmayogi digital platform for online training and continuous learning.
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question_text: The 'Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM)' recently in the news has been implemented by:

options.a: United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC)

questions.explanation: The CBAM is a climate policy tool adopted by the European Union (effective 2023, full implementation 2026). It puts a carbon price on imports from outside the EU in emission-intensive sectors (steel, cement, aluminium, fertilizers, electricity, hydrogen) to prevent carbon leakage.
questions.subject: Economy
